architecture.style: layered inference platform with multi-surface interfaces and an attached streaming extension
architecture.summary: The repository centers on a shared PyTorch inference core and fans it out into API, CLI, Gradio, backend-service, and long-video streaming execution paths, with utilities and exporters providing the connective tissue.
traits: Single inference core reused by multiple entry surfaces
Strong separation between model internals, orchestration services, UI modules, and export utilities
Research-heavy geometry and rendering helpers embedded in the product-facing package
Streaming subsystem extends the main package for chunked video and loop closure workflows

components.name: Core Public API
responsibilities: Load configurations, registries, and model assets
Accept input imagery through I/O helpers
Normalize predictions for export and downstream use

components.name: Operator Interfaces
responsibilities: Route user inputs into inference services
Support image, video, COLMAP, backend, and UI launch modes

components.name: Backend Service Layer
responsibilities: Keep a model loaded in memory
Expose task status and result retrieval endpoints

components.name: Model Stack
responsibilities: Provide DINOv2 vision backbones and DPT-style heads
Estimate camera features and adapt outputs for Gaussian splatting

components.name: Streaming Extension
responsibilities: Chunk long videos into manageable windows
Detect loops and align chunks with Sim3 methods
Produce large-sequence outputs with memory-aware processing

primary_path: User enters through README, CLI, Python API, Gradio app, or backend endpoint
Input handlers normalize images, videos, or COLMAP-style assets
The public API loads model configs, weights, and utility dependencies
Model components generate depth, confidence, pose, and related 3D predictions
Output processors and exporters materialize arrays, visualizations, meshes, or Gaussian-splat artifacts
Optional backend mode reuses a resident model to amortize GPU initialization cost
Optional streaming mode repeats inference across chunks and reconciles them with loop-aware alignment

handoffs.artifact: normalized local or backend execution request
loaded model call with processed inputs
prediction object with depth, pose, confidence, and geometry outputs

inputs: Single images, image batches, videos, or COLMAP reconstructions
Streaming configs for chunking and loop handling
intermediate_artifacts: Processed image tensors and camera representations
Depth, confidence, intrinsics, and extrinsics arrays
Geometry buffers, visualization frames, and task metadata
Chunk-level point clouds and Sim3 alignment state in streaming mode
outputs: NPZ, GLB, COLMAP, feature-visualization, and Gaussian-splat export assets
Interactive galleries and backend task results

internal: Interfaces call into a shared service and API core
Model modules depend heavily on shared geometry and spec utilities
UI modules remain mostly isolated from core inference internals except through orchestration helpers
Streaming utilities depend on the main API but maintain their own alignment toolchain
external: Hugging Face and safetensors model assets
Open3D, pycolmap, trimesh, and related 3D ecosystem packages

strengths: Well-defined separation between inference core and operator surfaces
Rich export pipeline makes predictions usable in downstream geometry workflows
Backend mode addresses repeated inference cost by keeping models warm
Streaming subsystem broadens the repo from image inference into long-video reconstruction
risks: The available input is a prepared snapshot rather than live source files, so some architectural details may be omitted.
Heavy dependency surface and GPU assumptions increase setup complexity.
Model, export, and utility layers are large enough that cross-module coupling may be difficult to evolve safely.
Streaming code appears specialized and may drift from the main package if contracts are not kept stable.
